scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

BETWEEN:

RAM KUMAR, S/o {{FATHER'S NAME}}, Aged about 45 years, R/o Jayanagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PINCODE}}. ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

SURESH PATEL, S/o {{FATHER'S NAME}}, Aged about 38 years, R/o Koramangala, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PINCODE}}. ... DEFENDANT

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

1. The Plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, and is competent to file this suit.

2. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, and is within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. The cause of action has arisen within the limits of this Hon'ble Court.

3. This Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the Defendant resides within the jurisdiction, and under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as this is a civil suit for recovery of money.

4. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) to the Defendant via NEFT bank transfer. The transaction is identified by UTR: AXIB20240315123456.

5. There was no written agreement executed between the Plaintiff and the Defendant regarding this loan. The transaction was based on mutual trust and oral understanding.

6. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount via WhatsApp messages. These digital communications confirm the debt owed by the Defendant to the Plaintiff.

7. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount as per the understanding. The date of default for the purpose of interest calculation is 20.06.2024.

8. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment of the outstanding amount.

9. Despite the legal notice and repeated oral demands, the Defendant has failed to repay the loan amount, leaving the Plaintiff with no option but to approach this Hon'ble Court.

STATEMENT OF ACCOUNT

10. The details of the account between the Plaintiff and the Defendant are as follows:

    Principal Amount Advanced: Rs. 15,00,000
    Date of Advance: 15.03.2024
    Part-Payments Received: Nil
    Balance Principal: Rs. 15,00,000
    Interest Claimed: 12% per annum from 20.06.2024
    Total Due as on Date of Filing: Rs. 15,00,000 + Accrued Interest

LEGAL BASIS

11. Under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation from the party who has broken the contract. The Defendant's failure to repay the loan constitutes a breach of the oral contract, entitling the Plaintiff to recover the principal amount.

12. Under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, Courts shall try all suits of a civil nature unless expressly barred. This suit for money recovery is a civil suit maintainable under this provision.

LIMITATION

13. This suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 113 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963.

14. The period is three years from the date when the right to sue accrued, which is the date of default (20.06.2024). The suit is filed well within the limitation period.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024, when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount as per the default date agreed upon for interest calculation.

16. The cause of action further arose on 01.07.2024, when the Plaintiff issued the legal notice demanding repayment and the Defendant failed to comply.

17. The cause of action continues to subsist as the amount remains unpaid.

INTEREST

18. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

19. The Plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum on the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000 from 20.06.2024 until realization. This includes pre-suit interest, pendente lite interest during the pendency of this suit, and future interest until final repayment.

COURT FEE

20. The appropriate court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on this plaint as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, based on the ad valorem rate applicable for the suit value.

PRAYER

21. The Plaintiff respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
    (a) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;
    (b) Award interest at 12% per annum on the principal amount from 20.06.2024 until realisation;
    (c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
    (d) Award future interest until the date of full payment;
    (e) Award costs of this suit in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant;
    (f) Grant such other and further reliefs as this Hon'ble Court deems fit in the circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

22. The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:
    ANNEXURE-A: Bank Transaction Record showing NEFT transfer with UTR: AXIB20240315123456 dated 15.03.
